1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: This is the beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
The prophet Isaiah wrote: I am sending my messenger ahead of you. He will prepare your way.
A voice is calling out in the desert: Get the way ready for the Lord. Make a straight road for him.
So John came, baptizing in the desert and preaching that people should turn away from their sins and be baptized, so their sins would be forgiven.
People from the whole land of Judea and the city of Jerusalem came out to John. They admitted their sins, and he baptized them in the Jordan River.
John wore clothes made of camel hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
This is what he preached: After me comes someone greater than I am. I am not good enough even to bend down and untie his sandals.
I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
At that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
As soon as Jesus came up out of the water, he saw heaven open and the Spirit coming down on him like a dove.
And a voice came from heaven: You are my Son, whom I love. I am very pleased with you.
Right away the Spirit sent Jesus out into the desert.
He was in the desert forty days, and Satan tempted him. He was with the wild animals, and angels took care of him.
After John was put in prison, Jesus went into Galilee, preaching the good news of God.
He said: The time has come. God's kingdom is near. Turn away from your sins and believe the good news.
As Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew. They were throwing a net into the water, because they were fishermen.
Jesus said to them: Come and follow me, and I will make you fishers of people.
At once they left their nets and followed him.
Going a little farther, Jesus saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
Right away he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers and followed Jesus.
They went to Capernaum. On the Sabbath, Jesus went into the synagogue and began to teach.
The people were amazed at his teaching, because he taught as one who had authority, not like the teachers of the law.
Just then a man with an evil spirit was in the synagogue. He cried out:
What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God!
Jesus ordered the spirit: Be quiet! Come out of him!
The evil spirit shook the man hard, gave a loud scream, and came out of him.
The people were all so amazed that they asked each other: What is this? A new teaching — and with such authority! He even gives orders to evil spirits, and they obey him.
News about Jesus spread quickly all over Galilee.
As soon as they left the synagogue, Jesus and his disciples went to the home of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
Simon's mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they told Jesus about her right away.
Jesus went to her, took her by the hand, and helped her up. The fever left her, and she began to serve them.
That evening, after the sun went down, the people brought to Jesus all who were sick and all who had demons.
The whole town gathered at the door.
Jesus healed many people who had different kinds of sickness. He also drove out many demons, but he would not let the demons speak, because they knew who he was.
Very early the next morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up and went off to a lonely place to pray.
Simon and the others went to look for him.
When they found him, they said: Everyone is looking for you!
Jesus answered: Let us go somewhere else, to the nearby towns, so I can preach there too. That is why I have come.
So he traveled all through Galilee, preaching in the synagogues and driving out demons.
A man with a skin disease came to Jesus and begged him on his knees: If you are willing, you can make me clean.
Jesus felt sorry for the man. He reached out his hand and touched him. He said: I am willing. Be clean!
At once the disease left the man, and he was clean.
Jesus sent him away with a strong warning:
See that you do not tell anyone. Go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the sacrifice that Moses commanded for your cleansing. This will be proof to the people.

But the man went out and began to talk freely, spreading the news everywhere. As a result, Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He stayed outside in lonely places, and people came to him from everywhere.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: A few days later, Jesus came back to Capernaum. People heard that he was home.
So many people gathered that there was no room left, not even outside the door. And Jesus preached the word to them.
Some men came, bringing a man who was paralyzed. Four men were carrying him.
But they could not get near Jesus because of the crowd. So they made a hole in the roof above Jesus, and they let the paralyzed man down on his mat.
When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man: Son, your sins are forgiven.
Some teachers of the law were sitting there. They were thinking to themselves:
Why does this man talk like this? He is insulting God! Only God can forgive sins.
Jesus knew right away what they were thinking. He said to them: Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?
Which is easier? To say to the paralyzed man, Your sins are forgiven? Or to say, Get up, take your mat, and walk?
But I will show you that the Son of Man has power on earth to forgive sins. So Jesus said to the paralyzed man:
I tell you, get up, take your mat, and go home.
The man got up at once, took his mat, and walked out in front of them all. Everyone was amazed and praised God. They said: We have never seen anything like this!
Jesus went out beside the lake again. A large crowd came to him, and he taught them.
As he walked along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth. Jesus said to him: Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
Later Jesus was eating at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples, for many of them were following him.
The teachers of the law who were Pharisees saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. They asked his disciples: Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?
When Jesus heard this, he said to them: Healthy people do not need a doctor. Sick people do. I did not come to invite good people. I came to invite sinners.
John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. Some people came to Jesus and asked: John's disciples fast, and the Pharisees' disciples fast. Why don't your disciples fast?
Jesus answered: The friends of the groom do not fast while the groom is still with them. As long as he is with them, they cannot fast.
But the time will come when the groom will be taken away from them. Then they will fast.
No one sews a piece of new cloth on an old coat. If he does, the new patch will pull away from the old, and the tear will be worse.
And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the skins will be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins.
One Sabbath day Jesus was walking through the grain fields. As his disciples walked, they began to pick some heads of grain.
The Pharisees said to him: Look! Why are they doing what is not allowed on the Sabbath?
Jesus answered: Have you never read what David did when he and his men were hungry and needed food?
It was when Abiathar was high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the holy bread, which only the priests were allowed to eat. And he also gave some to his men.
Then Jesus said to them: The Sabbath was made to help people. People were not made for the Sabbath.
So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Jesus went into the synagogue again. A man with a crippled hand was there.
Some people were watching Jesus closely. They wanted to see if he would heal the man on the Sabbath, so they could accuse him.
Jesus said to the man with the crippled hand: Stand up here in front of everyone.
Then Jesus asked the people: Is it right to do good on the Sabbath, or to do evil? To save a life, or to kill? But they said nothing.
Jesus looked around at them with anger. He was very sad because their hearts were so hard. He said to the man: Hold out your hand. The man held it out, and his hand was healed.
Then the Pharisees went out and began to plan with the followers of Herod how they could kill Jesus.
Jesus went away to the lake with his disciples. A large crowd from Galilee followed him.
Many people also came from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, the lands east of the Jordan River, and the areas around Tyre and Sidon. They came because they heard about the great things Jesus was doing.
Because of the crowd, Jesus told his disciples to have a small boat ready for him, so the people would not crush him.
He had healed so many that the sick people were pushing forward to touch him.
Whenever the evil spirits saw Jesus, they fell down in front of him and cried out: You are the Son of God!
But Jesus strongly warned them not to tell anyone who he was.
Then Jesus went up on a mountain. He called the men he wanted, and they came to him.
He chose twelve men and called them apostles. He wanted them to be with him and to send them out to preach,
and to have power to drive out demons.
These are the twelve he chose: Simon (Jesus gave him the name Peter),
James and his brother John, the sons of Zebedee (Jesus gave them the name Boanerges, which means Sons of Thunder),
Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot,
and Judas Iscariot, the one who later handed Jesus over to his enemies.
Then Jesus went home, and again a crowd gathered, so that Jesus and his disciples could not even eat.
When Jesus' family heard about it, they went to take charge of him. People were saying: He is out of his mind.
The teachers of the law who came down from Jerusalem said: Satan, the prince of demons, is in him. That is how he drives out demons.
So Jesus called them together and spoke to them in word pictures: How can Satan drive out Satan?
If a kingdom fights against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
If a family fights against itself, that family cannot stand.
And if Satan fights against himself, he cannot stand. That is the end of him.
No one can go into a strong man's house and steal his things unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can rob the house.
I tell you the truth: people can be forgiven for all their sins and for the evil things they say.
But anyone who speaks against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin that lasts forever.
Jesus said this because they were saying: He has an evil spirit.
Then Jesus' mother and brothers came. They stood outside and sent someone in to call him.
A crowd was sitting around Jesus. They told him: Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you.
Jesus answered: Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?
Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said: Look, here are my mother and my brothers!
Anyone who does what God wants is my brother and my sister and my mother.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Again Jesus began to teach beside the lake. A very large crowd gathered around him. So he got into a boat on the lake and sat down, while all the people stood on the shore.
He taught them many things using word pictures. He said:
Listen! A farmer went out to plant seed.
As he scattered the seed, some fell on the path. The birds came and ate it up.
Some seed fell on rocky ground, where there was not much soil. The seed grew quickly, because the soil was not deep.
But when the sun came up, the young plants were burned. They had no roots, so they dried up.
Some seed fell among thorns. The thorns grew up and choked the plants, so they did not produce grain.
But some seed fell on good soil. It grew up, became strong, and produced grain. Some plants made thirty times more, some sixty times more, and some a hundred times more.
Then Jesus said: Anyone who has ears should listen!
Later, when Jesus was alone, the twelve and the others around him asked him about the word pictures.
He told them: God has let you know the secret about his kingdom. But to those outside, everything is told in word pictures,
so that they may look and look but not see, and listen and listen but not understand. If they did, they might turn to God and be forgiven.
Then Jesus said to them: Do you not understand this word picture? Then how will you understand any of them?
The farmer plants the word of God in people.
Some people are like the seed on the path. As soon as they hear the word, Satan comes and takes away the word that was planted in them.
Others are like the seed on rocky ground. They hear the word and are glad to accept it right away.
But they have no root, so they last only a short time. When trouble or hard times come because of the word, they quickly give up.
Others are like the seed planted among thorns. They hear the word,
but the worries of this life, the love of money, and the desire for other things crowd in and choke the word, so it does not produce anything.
But others are like the seed planted on good soil. They hear the word, accept it, and produce a good harvest — thirty, sixty, or a hundred times more.
Then Jesus said to them: Do you bring in a lamp and put it under a bowl or under the bed? No, you put it on a lampstand.
Everything that is hidden will be brought out into the open. Every secret will be made known.
Anyone who has ears should listen!
Then he said: Think carefully about what you hear. The way you treat others is the way God will treat you, and he will give you even more.
The person who has something will be given more. But the person who has nothing will lose even the little he has.
Jesus also said: God's kingdom is like a man who plants seed in the ground.
Night and day, whether he is asleep or awake, the seed grows. He does not know how it happens.
The soil makes the plant grow by itself. First the blade comes up, then the head, then the full grain in the head.
As soon as the grain is ready, the man cuts it, because it is harvest time.
Then Jesus said: What can we compare God's kingdom to? What word picture can we use?
It is like a mustard seed. It is the smallest of all seeds you plant in the ground.
But after it is planted, it grows up and becomes bigger than all the garden plants. It grows large branches, so that the birds can rest in its shade.
Jesus used many word pictures like these to teach the people. He taught them as much as they could understand.
He always used word pictures when he spoke to them. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them.
That evening Jesus said to his disciples: Let us go across to the other side of the lake.
They left the crowd and took Jesus along in the boat, just as he was. Other boats were with him too.
A strong wind began to blow. The waves came up into the boat, so that it was almost full of water.
Jesus was in the back of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. The disciples woke him up and said: Teacher, don't you care that we are going to drown?
Jesus got up and ordered the wind to stop. He said to the waves: Be quiet! Be still! Then the wind stopped, and everything was perfectly calm.
Jesus said to his disciples: Why are you afraid? Do you still have no faith?
They were very afraid. They said to one another: Who is this man? Even the wind and the waves obey him!

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the area of the Gerasenes.
As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man with an evil spirit came out from the tombs to meet him.
This man lived among the tombs. No one could tie him up anymore, not even with chains.
Many times his hands and feet had been tied with chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the irons on his feet. No one was strong enough to control him.
Night and day he stayed among the tombs and in the hills, screaming and cutting himself with stones.
When the man saw Jesus far away, he ran and bowed down in front of him.
He shouted with a loud voice: What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Promise God that you will not hurt me!
He said this because Jesus had said to him: Come out of this man, you evil spirit!
Then Jesus asked him: What is your name? The man answered: My name is Legion, because there are many of us.
And he begged Jesus again and again not to send them out of that area.
A large herd of pigs was feeding nearby on the hill.
The evil spirits begged Jesus: Send us to the pigs. Let us go into them.
Jesus allowed them. So the evil spirits came out of the man and went into the pigs. The whole herd — about two thousand pigs — rushed down the steep hill into the lake and drowned.
The men who took care of the pigs ran away. They told the news in the town and in the countryside, and the people came to see what had happened.
They came to Jesus and saw the man who had been controlled by the demons. He was sitting there, dressed and in his right mind. And the people were afraid.
The ones who had seen it told the others what had happened to the man and to the pigs.
Then the people began to beg Jesus to leave their area.
As Jesus was getting into the boat, the man who had been set free begged to go with him.
But Jesus would not let him. He said: Go home to your family, and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how kind he has been to you.
So the man went away and began to tell everyone in the Ten Towns how much Jesus had done for him. And everyone was amazed.
Jesus went back across the lake in the boat. A large crowd gathered around him by the shore.
A leader of the synagogue named Jairus came there. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
He begged Jesus again and again: My little daughter is dying. Please come and put your hands on her, so she will be healed and live.
So Jesus went with him. A large crowd followed and pushed close around Jesus.
A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
She had suffered very much under the care of many doctors. She had spent all her money, but she was not better. Instead, she was getting worse.
She had heard about Jesus. So she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his coat.
She thought: If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.
As soon as she touched him, her bleeding stopped. She could feel in her body that she was healed.
At once Jesus knew that power had gone out of him. He turned around in the crowd and asked: Who touched my clothes?
His disciples said: You can see the crowd pushing against you. Why do you ask who touched you?
But Jesus kept looking around to see who had done it.
Then the woman, shaking with fear, came and fell down in front of Jesus. She knew what had happened to her, and she told him the whole truth.
Jesus said to her: Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace. You are free from your suffering.
While Jesus was still speaking, some men came from the house of Jairus, the synagogue leader. They said: Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher anymore?
But Jesus paid no attention to what they said. He told Jairus: Do not be afraid. Just believe.
Jesus let no one go with him except Peter, James, and John, the brother of James.
They came to the house of the synagogue leader. Jesus saw the people crying loudly and very upset.
He went inside and said to them: Why all this crying and noise? The child is not dead. She is only sleeping.
But the people laughed at him. So Jesus sent them all outside. He took the child's father and mother and his three disciples, and went in to where the child was.
He took her by the hand and said to her: Talitha koum! This means: Little girl, I say to you, get up!
At once the girl stood up and walked around. She was twelve years old. The people were completely amazed.
Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell anyone about it. Then he told them to give her something to eat.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Jesus left there and went to his hometown, and his disciples followed him.

On the Sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue. Many people who heard him were amazed. They said: Where did this man get all this? What is this wisdom that has been given to him? How does he do such miracles?
Is this not the carpenter, the son of Mary and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Are not his sisters here with us? And the people would not accept him.
But Jesus said to them: A prophet is honored everywhere except in his own hometown, among his relatives, and in his own house.
So Jesus could not do any miracles there, except to put his hands on a few sick people and heal them.
He was amazed that they did not believe. Then Jesus went around to the villages, teaching the people.
Jesus called the twelve to him and began to send them out two by two. He gave them power over evil spirits.
He told them to take nothing for the trip except a walking stick — no bread, no bag, no money in their belts.
They could wear sandals but not take an extra coat.
He said to them: When you go into a house, stay there until you leave that town.
And if any place will not welcome you or listen to you, leave that place and shake the dust off your feet as a warning to them.
So they went out and preached that people should turn away from their sins.
They drove out many demons and put olive oil on many sick people and healed them.
King Herod heard about Jesus, because Jesus' name had become well known. Some people were saying: John the Baptist has come back from the dead. That is why he has power to do miracles.
Others said: He is Elijah. And still others said: He is a prophet, like the prophets long ago.
But when Herod heard this, he said: This is John, the man I had beheaded. He has come back from the dead!
Herod himself had given the order to arrest John. He had John tied up and put in prison. He did this because of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. Herod had married her,
and John had been telling Herod: It is not right for you to have your brother's wife.
So Herodias hated John and wanted to kill him. But she could not,
because Herod was afraid of John. Herod knew that John was a good and holy man, so he protected him. When Herod listened to John, it troubled him, yet he liked to listen to him.
Finally the right time came. On his birthday Herod gave a banquet for his officials, his army commanders, and the leading men of Galilee.
The daughter of Herodias came in and danced, and she pleased Herod and his guests. The king said to the girl: Ask me for anything you want, and I will give it to you.
He promised her: Whatever you ask, I will give it to you, even half my kingdom.
She went out and asked her mother: What should I ask for? Her mother answered: The head of John the Baptist.
At once the girl hurried back to the king and said: I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptist on a plate.
The king was very sad. But because of the promise he had made in front of his guests, he did not want to refuse her.
So the king sent a soldier at once and ordered him to bring John's head. The soldier went and beheaded John in the prison.
He brought John's head on a plate and gave it to the girl, and the girl gave it to her mother.
When John's disciples heard about it, they came and took his body and laid it in a tomb.
The apostles came back to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
There were so many people coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have time to eat. So Jesus said to them: Come away with me to a quiet place and rest a while.
So they went away by themselves in a boat to a quiet place.
But many people saw them leaving and knew where they were going. So they ran on foot from all the towns and got there ahead of them.
When Jesus came ashore, he saw the large crowd. He felt sorry for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. So he began to teach them many things.
By now it was getting late. His disciples came to him and said: This is a lonely place, and it is already very late.
Send the people away, so they can go to the farms and villages around here and buy themselves something to eat.
But Jesus answered: You give them something to eat. They said to him: Do you want us to go and spend a small fortune on bread to feed them?
Jesus asked: How many loaves of bread do you have? Go and see. When they found out, they said: Five loaves and two fish.
Then Jesus told them to have all the people sit down in groups on the green grass.
So they sat down in groups of a hundred and groups of fifty.

Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up to heaven and thanked God. Then he broke the loaves and gave them to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
Everyone ate and was full.
Afterward the disciples picked up twelve baskets full of leftover bread and fish.
There were about five thousand men who had eaten.
Right away Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and go ahead of him to Bethsaida, while he sent the people away.
After he had sent them away, he went up on a mountain to pray.
When evening came, the boat was in the middle of the lake, and Jesus was alone on the land.
He saw that the disciples were having trouble rowing, because the wind was against them. Some time before dawn, Jesus came to them, walking on the water. He was about to walk past them,
but when they saw him walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost. They cried out,
because they all saw him and were terrified. But Jesus spoke to them right away. He said: Be brave! It is I. Do not be afraid.
Then he climbed into the boat with them, and the wind stopped. They were completely amazed,
because they still did not understand about the loaves. Their hearts were hard.
When they had crossed the lake, they came to the shore at Gennesaret and tied up the boat.
As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
They ran through that whole area, carrying the sick on mats to wherever they heard he was.

Wherever Jesus went — into villages, towns, or the countryside — the people laid the sick in the marketplaces. They begged him to let them touch even the edge of his coat, and all who touched him were healed.

7.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: The Pharisees and some teachers of the law came from Jerusalem and gathered around Jesus.
They saw that some of his disciples were eating with hands that were not clean — that is, they had not washed them.
(The Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they wash their hands in a special way, following the teaching of their elders.
And when they come from the market, they do not eat unless they wash. They also follow many other rules, such as the washing of cups, pots, and kettles.)
So the Pharisees and teachers of the law asked Jesus: Why do your disciples not follow the teaching of the elders? Why do they eat with hands that are not clean?
Jesus answered: Isaiah was right when he spoke about you who only pretend to be good. He wrote: These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
Their worship of me means nothing. They teach human rules as if they were God's commands.
You have let go of God's commands and hold on to human rules.
Then Jesus said to them: You are very good at setting aside God's commands so you can keep your own rules!
Moses said: Honor your father and your mother. And: Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.
But you say that a person may tell his father or mother: Whatever I could have used to help you is Corban (that means, it is a gift given to God).
In that way you no longer let him do anything for his father or mother.
So you cancel the word of God by your own rules, which you pass on to others. And you do many things like that.
Again Jesus called the crowd to him and said: Everyone listen to me, and understand this.
Nothing that goes into a person from the outside can make him unclean. It is what comes out of a person that makes him unclean.
Anyone who has ears should listen!
After Jesus left the crowd and went into the house, his disciples asked him about this word picture.
He said to them: Do you still not understand? Don't you see that nothing that goes into a person from the outside can make him unclean?
It does not go into his heart but into his stomach, and then it passes out of his body. (By saying this, Jesus made all foods clean.)
Then he said: What comes out of a person is what makes him unclean.
For from the inside, out of a person's heart, come evil thoughts, sexual sin, stealing, murder,
adultery, greed, doing wrong to others, lying, wild living, jealousy, insulting others, pride, and foolishness.
All these evil things come from inside a person and make him unclean.
Jesus left there and went to the area around Tyre. He went into a house and did not want anyone to know he was there. But he could not stay hidden.
A woman whose little daughter had an evil spirit heard about Jesus. She came and fell at his feet.
The woman was a Greek, born in Syrian Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to drive the demon out of her daughter.
Jesus told her: First let the children eat all they want, for it is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.
She answered: Yes, Lord, but even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs.
Then Jesus told her: Because you said this, you may go. The demon has left your daughter.
She went home and found her child lying on the bed, and the demon was gone.
Then Jesus left the area around Tyre and went through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, into the area of the Ten Towns.
Some people brought him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus to put his hand on the man.
Jesus took him away from the crowd, to be alone with him. He put his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and touched the man's tongue.
Jesus looked up to heaven, took a deep breath, and said to the man: Ephphatha! (This means, Be opened.)
At once the man could hear, and his tongue was set free, and he began to speak clearly.
Jesus told the people not to tell anyone. But the more he told them, the more they kept talking about it.
The people were completely amazed. They said: He has done everything well. He even makes the deaf hear and those who cannot speak talk.

8.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: During those days another large crowd gathered. The people had nothing to eat. So Jesus called his disciples to him and said:
I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me three days now and have nothing to eat.
If I send them home hungry, they will faint on the way, because some of them have come a long way.
His disciples answered: Where could anyone get enough bread to feed them here in this lonely place?
Jesus asked: How many loaves do you have? They answered: Seven.
Jesus told the people to sit down on the ground. Then he took the seven loaves and gave thanks to God. He broke the loaves and gave them to his disciples to give to the people, and they did.
They also had a few small fish. Jesus gave thanks for them too and told the disciples to give them to the people.
The people ate and were full. Afterward the disciples picked up seven baskets of leftover pieces.
About four thousand people were there. After Jesus sent them away,
he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the area of Dalmanutha.
The Pharisees came and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
Jesus took a deep breath and said: Why do these people ask for a sign? I tell you the truth, no sign will be given to them.
Then he left them, got back into the boat, and went to the other side of the lake.
The disciples had forgotten to bring bread. They had only one loaf with them in the boat.
Jesus warned them: Be careful. Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod.
The disciples talked about this with each other and said: He says this because we have no bread.
Jesus knew what they were saying, so he asked them: Why are you talking about having no bread? Do you still not see or understand? Are your hearts hard?
You have eyes — can't you see? You have ears — can't you hear? Don't you remember?
When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets of leftover pieces did you pick up? They answered: Twelve.
And when I broke the seven loaves for the four thousand, how many baskets of leftover pieces did you pick up? They answered: Seven.
Then he said to them: Do you still not understand?
They came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch the man.
Jesus took the blind man by the hand and led him outside the village. Then he spit on the man's eyes and put his hands on him. Jesus asked: Can you see anything?
The man looked up and said: I see people, but they look like trees walking around.
Once more Jesus put his hands on the man's eyes. Then the man's eyes were opened. His sight came back, and he could see everything clearly.
Jesus sent him home and said: Do not even go into the village.
Jesus and his disciples went to the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked them: Who do people say I am?
They answered: Some say John the Baptist. Others say Elijah. And still others say you are one of the prophets.
But what about you? Jesus asked. Who do you say I am? Peter answered: You are the Christ.
Jesus warned them not to tell anyone about him.

Then Jesus began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things. He would be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the law. He would be killed, and after three days he would rise again.
Jesus spoke about this plainly. Then Peter took him aside and began to argue with him.
But Jesus turned around, looked at his disciples, and corrected Peter. He said: Get behind me, Satan! You are not thinking about the things of God, but about the things of people.
Then Jesus called the crowd to him, along with his disciples, and said: If anyone wants to follow me, he must say no to himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
For whoever wants to save his life will lose it. But whoever loses his life for me and for the good news will save it.
What good is it for someone to gain the whole world but lose his soul?
Or what can a person give to buy back his soul?
If anyone is ashamed of me and my words among these sinful people, then the Son of Man will be ashamed of him when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.

9.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then Jesus said to them: I tell you the truth, some of you standing here will not die before you see God's kingdom come with power.
Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John up a high mountain by themselves. There Jesus was changed in front of them.
His clothes became shining white, whiter than anyone on earth could make them.
Then Elijah and Moses appeared, and they were talking with Jesus.
Peter said to Jesus: Teacher, it is good for us to be here. Let us put up three shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
Peter did not know what to say, because he and the others were so afraid.
Then a cloud came and covered them, and a voice came from the cloud: This is my Son, whom I love. Listen to him!
They looked around, and suddenly they saw no one with them except Jesus.
As they came down the mountain, Jesus told them not to tell anyone what they had seen until the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
They kept this to themselves, but they asked each other what rising from the dead meant.
Then they asked Jesus: Why do the teachers of the law say that Elijah must come first?
Jesus answered: Elijah does come first to make everything ready. But why is it written that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be treated like nothing?
I tell you, Elijah has already come, and they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it is written about him.
When Jesus and the three disciples came back to the others, they saw a large crowd around them, and the teachers of the law were arguing with them.
As soon as the people saw Jesus, they were amazed and ran to greet him.
Jesus asked: What are you arguing about with them?
A man in the crowd answered: Teacher, I brought my son to you. He has an evil spirit that keeps him from talking.
Whenever the spirit takes hold of him, it throws him to the ground. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and becomes stiff. I asked your disciples to drive out the spirit, but they could not.
Jesus said: You people have no faith! How long must I stay with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy to me.
So they brought the boy. As soon as the spirit saw Jesus, it threw the boy into a fit. He fell to the ground, rolling around and foaming at the mouth.
Jesus asked the boy's father: How long has this been happening to him? The father answered: Since he was a child.
The spirit has often thrown him into fire or water to kill him. But if you can do anything, please feel sorry for us and help us.
Jesus said: If you can? Everything is possible for the one who believes.
At once the boy's father cried out: I do believe! Help me with my unbelief!
When Jesus saw that a crowd was running up, he ordered the evil spirit: You spirit that makes this boy unable to hear or speak, I command you, come out of him and never go into him again!
The spirit screamed, shook the boy hard, and came out. The boy looked like a dead body, so that many said: He is dead.
But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and the boy stood up.
Later, when Jesus was inside the house, his disciples asked him in private: Why couldn't we drive out the spirit?
Jesus answered: This kind can come out only by prayer and fasting.
They left there and went through Galilee. Jesus did not want anyone to know where they were,
because he was teaching his disciples. He told them: The Son of Man will be handed over to men. They will kill him, and three days later he will rise again.
But they did not understand what he meant, and they were afraid to ask him.
They came to Capernaum. When they were in the house, Jesus asked them: What were you arguing about on the road?
But they said nothing, because on the road they had argued about which of them was the greatest.
Jesus sat down and called the twelve to him. He said: If anyone wants to be first, he must be the very last, and the servant of everyone.
Then he took a little child and stood the child among them. He took the child in his arms and said to them:
Anyone who welcomes a little child like this in my name welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me does not welcome only me, but also the One who sent me.
John said to him: Teacher, we saw a man driving out demons in your name, and we told him to stop, because he is not one of us.
But Jesus said: Do not stop him. No one who does a miracle in my name can soon afterward say anything bad about me.
Anyone who is not against us is for us.
I tell you the truth, anyone who gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will surely be rewarded.
If anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in me to sin, it would be better for him to be thrown into the sea with a large stone tied around his neck.
If your hand causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better to enter life without a hand than to have two hands and go to hell, where the fire never goes out.
There the worm does not die, and the fire is never put out.
And if your foot causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better to enter life crippled than to have two feet and be thrown into hell, where the fire never goes out.
There the worm does not die, and the fire is never put out.
And if your eye causes you to sin, take it out. It is better to enter God's kingdom with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell,
where the worm does not die and the fire is never put out.
Everyone will be made pure by fire, the way a sacrifice is made pure by salt.
Salt is good. But if it loses its taste, how can you make it salty again? Keep salt in yourselves, and live in peace with one another.

10.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then Jesus left there and went into the area of Judea and across the Jordan River. Again crowds came to him, and as usual he taught them.
Some Pharisees came to test Jesus. They asked: Is it right for a man to divorce his wife?
Jesus answered: What did Moses command you?
They said: Moses allowed a man to write a paper of divorce and send his wife away.
Jesus said to them: Moses wrote that rule because your hearts were hard.
But at the beginning, when God made the world, he made them male and female.
For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,
and the two will become one. So they are no longer two, but one.
No one should separate what God has joined together.
Later, in the house, the disciples asked Jesus about this again.
He told them: Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman is guilty of adultery against her.
And if a woman divorces her husband and marries another man, she is guilty of adultery.
People were bringing little children to Jesus so he could touch them. But the disciples told them to stop.
When Jesus saw this, he was upset. He said to them: Let the little children come to me. Do not stop them, because God's kingdom belongs to people like these.
I tell you the truth, anyone who does not receive God's kingdom like a little child will never enter it.
Then Jesus took the children in his arms, put his hands on them, and blessed them.
As Jesus was starting on his way, a man ran up and knelt before him. He asked: Good Teacher, what must I do to receive eternal life?
Jesus said: Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.
You know the commands: Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not lie, do not cheat, honor your father and mother.
The man said: Teacher, I have obeyed all these since I was a boy.

Jesus looked at him and loved him. He said: There is one thing you still need to do. Go, sell everything you have, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. After that, come and follow me.
When the man heard this, his face fell, and he went away sad, because he was very rich.
Jesus looked around and said to his disciples: How hard it is for rich people to enter God's kingdom!
The disciples were surprised at his words. But Jesus said again: Children, how hard it is to enter God's kingdom!
It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter God's kingdom.
The disciples were even more amazed. They said to each other: Then who can be saved?
Jesus looked at them and said: For people this is impossible, but not for God. Everything is possible for God.
Then Peter said to him: We have left everything to follow you.
Jesus answered: I tell you the truth, everyone who has left home, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or fields for me and for the good news
will receive a hundred times as much in this life — homes, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and fields, along with troubles — and in the world to come, eternal life.
But many who are first will be last, and many who are last will be first.

They were on the road, going up to Jerusalem. Jesus walked ahead of them. The disciples were amazed, and the others who followed were afraid. Again Jesus took the twelve aside and told them what was going to happen to him.

He said: We are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the law. They will say he must die, and they will hand him over to those who are not Jews.
They will make fun of him, spit on him, beat him with whips, and kill him. But three days later he will rise again.
Then James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to Jesus. They said: Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
Jesus said: What do you want me to do for you?
They answered: Let one of us sit at your right and the other at your left in your glory.
But Jesus said: You do not know what you are asking. Can you drink the cup I am going to drink? Can you be baptized with the baptism I am going to go through?
They answered: We can. Jesus said to them: You will drink the cup I drink, and you will go through the baptism I go through.
But it is not for me to say who will sit at my right or my left. Those places belong to those for whom they have been prepared.
When the other ten disciples heard about this, they were angry with James and John.
So Jesus called them together and said: You know that the rulers of the nations lord it over the people, and the important men use their power over them.
But it must not be that way among you. Whoever wants to be great among you must be your servant.
And whoever wants to be first must be the slave of everyone.
For even the Son of Man did not come to be served. He came to serve and to give his life to set many people free.
Then they came to Jericho. As Jesus and his disciples and a large crowd were leaving the town, a blind man named Bartimaeus, the son of Timaeus, was sitting by the road begging.
When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to shout: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!
Many people told him to be quiet. But he shouted even louder: Son of David, have mercy on me!
Jesus stopped and said: Call him. So they called the blind man and said: Cheer up! Get up! He is calling you.
The man threw off his coat, jumped up, and came to Jesus.
Jesus asked him: What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said: Teacher, I want to see.
Jesus said: Go, your faith has healed you. At once the man could see, and he followed Jesus down the road.

11.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: As they came near Jerusalem, to the towns of Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two of his disciples ahead.
He told them: Go into the village in front of you. As soon as you enter it, you will find a young donkey tied up, one that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
If anyone asks you, Why are you doing this?, tell him: The Lord needs it, and he will send it back here soon.
So they went and found a young donkey tied up outside a door, where two streets met, and they untied it.
Some people standing there asked them: What are you doing, untying that donkey?
They answered just as Jesus had told them, and the people let them go.
They brought the donkey to Jesus and threw their coats over it, and Jesus sat on it.
Many people spread their coats on the road, while others spread branches they had cut in the fields.
The people walking ahead and those following behind shouted: Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David! Hosanna in the highest heaven!
Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. He looked around at everything, but since it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
The next day, as they were leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
He saw in the distance a fig tree covered with leaves, so he went to see if it had any fruit. But when he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, because it was not the season for figs.
Then Jesus said to the tree: May no one ever eat fruit from you again. And his disciples heard him say it.

When they came to Jerusalem, Jesus went into the temple. He began to drive out the people who were buying and selling there. He turned over the tables of the money changers and the benches of those who were selling doves.
He would not allow anyone to carry goods through the temple.
Then he taught them: Is it not written: My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But you have made it a hideout for robbers.
The chief priests and the teachers of the law heard this and began looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the whole crowd was amazed at his teaching.
When evening came, Jesus and his disciples left the city.
The next morning, as they walked along, they saw the fig tree dried up all the way to the roots.
Peter remembered and said to Jesus: Teacher, look! The fig tree you cursed has dried up.
Jesus answered: Have faith in God.
I tell you the truth, if anyone says to this mountain, Go and throw yourself into the sea, and does not doubt in his heart but believes that what he says will happen, it will be done for him.
So I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
And when you stand praying, if you have anything against anyone, forgive him, so that your Father in heaven will also forgive your sins.
But if you do not forgive others, your Father in heaven will not forgive your sins.
They arrived again in Jerusalem. As Jesus was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the teachers of the law, and the elders came to him.
They asked: By what right are you doing these things? Who gave you the right to do them?
Jesus answered: I will ask you one question. Answer me, and then I will tell you by what right I do these things.
John's baptism — was it from heaven, or from people? Answer me.
They talked it over among themselves. They said: If we say, From heaven, he will ask, Then why didn't you believe him?
But if we say, From people... They were afraid of the crowd, because everyone believed that John really was a prophet.
So they answered Jesus: We do not know. Then Jesus said: Then I will not tell you by what right I do these things.

12.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then Jesus began to teach them with a word picture: A man planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a watchtower. Then he rented the vineyard to some farmers and went away to another country.
At harvest time he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit.
But the farmers grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away with nothing.
So the man sent another servant. They hit this one on the head and treated him shamefully.
He sent another, and they killed him. He sent many others. Some they beat, and some they killed.
The man had one person left to send — his own dear son. Last of all he sent him, thinking: They will respect my son.
But the farmers said to each other: This is the one who will inherit the vineyard. Come, let us kill him, and the vineyard will be ours.
So they grabbed the son, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
What will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and kill those farmers and give the vineyard to others.
Have you not read this Scripture? The stone the builders threw away has become the most important stone.
The Lord did this, and it is wonderful in our eyes.
The leaders knew that Jesus had told this word picture against them. So they wanted to arrest him, but they were afraid of the crowd. So they left him and went away.
Later they sent some Pharisees and some followers of Herod to Jesus, to trap him with his own words.

They came and said to him: Teacher, we know you are honest. You are not afraid of anyone, because you do not care who they are. You teach the way of God truthfully. Tell us: is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay or not?
But Jesus knew they were only pretending. He said to them: Why are you trying to trap me? Bring me a coin and let me look at it.
They brought one, and he asked them: Whose picture is this? Whose name is on it? They answered: Caesar's.
Then Jesus said to them: Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar, and give to God what belongs to God. And they were amazed at him.
Then some Sadducees came to Jesus. (The Sadducees say that people do not rise from the dead.) They asked him:
Teacher, Moses wrote that if a man dies and leaves a wife but no children, his brother must marry the widow and have children for him.
Now there were seven brothers. The first one married and died without children.
The second brother married the widow, but he also died without children. The same thing happened to the third.
None of the seven brothers had any children. Last of all, the woman died too.
Now when people rise from the dead, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
Jesus answered: You are wrong, because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God.
When people rise from the dead, they will not marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
Now about the dead rising — have you not read in the book of Moses, in the story of the burning bush, how God said to him: I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?
He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are badly mistaken.
One of the teachers of the law came and heard them arguing. He saw that Jesus had given them a good answer, so he asked: Which command is the most important of all?
Jesus answered: The most important command is this: Listen, Israel! The Lord our God is the one and only Lord.
Love the Lord your God with all your heart, all your soul, all your mind, and all your strength.
The second command is this: Love your neighbor as you love yourself. No command is greater than these.
The man said: Well said, Teacher. You are right that God is one and that there is no other besides him.
And to love him with all your heart, all your understanding, and all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself — this is more important than all the burnt offerings and sacrifices.
Jesus saw that the man had answered wisely. He said to him: You are not far from God's kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask Jesus any more questions.
While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he asked: Why do the teachers of the law say that the Christ is the son of David?
David himself, speaking by the Holy Spirit, said: The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet.
David himself calls him Lord. So how can the Christ be his son? And the large crowd listened to Jesus gladly.
As Jesus taught, he said: Watch out for the teachers of the law. They like to walk around in long robes and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces.
They love the most important seats in the synagogues and the best places at feasts.
They cheat widows out of their homes, and then they say long prayers to look good. These men will be punished all the more.
Jesus sat down across from the temple money box and watched the crowd putting in their money. Many rich people put in large amounts.
Then a poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, worth only a penny.
Jesus called his disciples to him and said: I tell you the truth, this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
They all gave out of what they had to spare. But she, out of her poverty, gave everything she had — all she had to live on.

13.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him: Teacher, look! What huge stones! What wonderful buildings!
Jesus replied: Do you see all these great buildings? Not one stone here will be left on another. Every one will be thrown down.
Later Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him in private:
Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are all about to come true?
Jesus said to them: Be careful that no one fools you.
Many will come using my name. They will say: I am the Christ. And they will fool many people.
When you hear about wars and reports of wars, do not be afraid. These things must happen, but the end is still to come.
Nation will fight against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in many places, and there will be times with no food. These are only the beginning of the troubles.
Be on your guard. People will hand you over to the courts, and you will be beaten in the synagogues. You will have to stand before rulers and kings because of me, to tell them the truth.
And the good news must first be preached to all nations.
When they arrest you and put you on trial, do not worry ahead of time about what to say. Just say whatever you are given to say at that time, because it will not be you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
Brother will hand over brother to be killed, and a father will hand over his child. Children will turn against their parents and have them put to death.
Everyone will hate you because of me. But the one who stands strong to the end will be saved.
You will see the terrible thing that destroys standing where it should not be. (Let the reader understand.) When you see this, the people in Judea should run to the mountains.
A person on the roof of his house should not go down or go inside to take anything out.
A person in the field should not go back to get his coat.
How hard it will be in those days for women who are pregnant or nursing babies!
Pray that this will not happen in winter.
Those will be terrible days — worse than any since the beginning, when God made the world, and worse than any that will ever come again.
If the Lord had not made those days shorter, no one would be saved. But for the sake of his chosen people, he has made those days shorter.
At that time, if anyone says to you: Look, here is the Christ! or, Look, there he is! — do not believe it.
For false Christs and false prophets will appear. They will do signs and wonders to fool people, even God's chosen ones, if that were possible.
So be on your guard. I have told you everything ahead of time.
In those days, after that terrible time, the sun will become dark, and the moon will not give its light.
The stars will fall from the sky, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
Then people will see the Son of Man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
And he will send his angels to gather his chosen people from every direction, from one end of the earth and sky to the other.
Learn a lesson from the fig tree. When its branches become soft and the leaves begin to grow, you know that summer is near.
In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that the time is near, right at the door.
I tell you the truth, the people living now will not all die before these things happen.
Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
No one knows the day or the hour, not even the angels in heaven, not even the Son. Only the Father knows.
Be on your guard! Stay awake! You do not know when the time will come.
It is like a man who goes away on a trip. He leaves his house, puts his servants in charge — each with his own work — and tells the one at the door to keep watch.
So keep watch, because you do not know when the owner of the house will come back — in the evening, at midnight, when the rooster crows, or at dawn.
If he comes suddenly, do not let him find you sleeping.
What I say to you, I say to everyone: Keep watch!

14.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: It was now two days before the Passover and the Feast of Bread Without Yeast. The chief priests and the teachers of the law were looking for a sneaky way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
But they said: Not during the feast, or the people may riot.

Jesus was in Bethany, eating at the home of Simon, a man who had once had a skin disease. A woman came with a jar of expensive perfume made of pure nard. She broke the jar and poured the perfume on Jesus' head.
Some of the people there were upset. They said to each other: Why waste this perfume?
It could have been sold for a large sum of money and given to the poor. And they spoke against the woman.
But Jesus said: Leave her alone. Why are you troubling her? She has done a beautiful thing for me.
You will always have the poor with you, and you can help them whenever you want. But you will not always have me.
She did what she could. She poured perfume on my body ahead of time, to prepare it for burial.
I tell you the truth, wherever the good news is preached in all the world, what this woman did will be told, so people will remember her.
Then Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went to the chief priests to hand Jesus over to them.
They were glad to hear this and promised to give him money. So Judas began looking for a good chance to hand Jesus over.
On the first day of the Feast of Bread Without Yeast, when the Passover lamb was killed, the disciples asked Jesus: Where do you want us to go and prepare the Passover meal for you?
Jesus sent two of his disciples and said: Go into the city. A man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him.
When he goes into a house, say to the owner: The Teacher asks, Where is my guest room, where I may eat the Passover meal with my disciples?
He will show you a large upstairs room, set up and ready. Prepare the meal for us there.
The disciples left, went into the city, and found everything just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover meal.
When evening came, Jesus arrived with the twelve.
While they were at the table eating, Jesus said: I tell you the truth, one of you eating with me will hand me over.
They became sad, and one after another they asked him: Surely you don't mean me?
Jesus answered: It is one of the twelve, one who is dipping his bread into the bowl with me.
The Son of Man will die, just as the Scriptures say. But how terrible it will be for the man who hands over the Son of Man! It would be better for that man if he had never been born.
While they were eating, Jesus took bread and thanked God for it. He broke it, gave it to them, and said: Take it. This is my body.
Then he took a cup, thanked God, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
He said to them: This is my blood, which begins the new agreement. It is poured out for many people.
I tell you the truth, I will not drink wine again until the day I drink it new in God's kingdom.
After they sang a song, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
Jesus told them: You will all turn away from me, because it is written: I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.
But after I rise from the dead, I will go ahead of you into Galilee.
Peter said: Even if everyone else turns away, I will not.
Jesus answered: I tell you the truth, today — this very night — before the rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you do not know me.
But Peter said even more strongly: Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I do not know you. And all the others said the same.
They went to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to his disciples: Sit here while I pray.
He took Peter, James, and John with him, and he began to feel deep trouble and great sorrow.
He said to them: My heart is so full of sorrow that I feel I could die. Stay here and keep watch.
Going a little farther, Jesus fell to the ground and prayed that, if it were possible, this hour might pass from him.
He said: Abba, Father, everything is possible for you. Take this cup away from me. But do not do what I want; do what you want.
Then Jesus came back and found the disciples sleeping. He said to Peter: Simon, are you asleep? Could you not keep watch for one hour?
Stay awake and pray, so that you will not give in to temptation. The spirit is willing, but the body is weak.
Again Jesus went away and prayed the same thing.
When he came back, he found them sleeping again, because they could not keep their eyes open. They did not know what to say to him.
He came back a third time and said: Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough! The time has come. Look, the Son of Man is about to be handed over to sinners.
Get up, let us go. Here comes the man who is handing me over.

Right away, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, arrived. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs, sent by the chief priests, the teachers of the law, and the elders.
The man who was handing Jesus over had given them a signal: The one I kiss is the man. Arrest him and lead him away under guard.
As soon as Judas arrived, he went straight to Jesus and said: Teacher! And he kissed him.
Then the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
One of the people standing nearby pulled out a sword and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his ear.
Jesus said to them: Am I leading a rebellion, that you have come with swords and clubs to arrest me?
Every day I was with you, teaching in the temple, and you did not arrest me. But this is happening so that the Scriptures will come true.
Then everyone left him and ran away.
A young man, wearing only a linen cloth, was following Jesus. When they grabbed him,
he left the cloth behind and ran away naked.
They led Jesus to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and teachers of the law came together.
Peter followed at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. There he sat with the guards and warmed himself by the fire.
The chief priests and the whole council were looking for evidence against Jesus so they could put him to death, but they found none.
Many gave false witness against him, but their stories did not agree.
Then some stood up and gave this false witness against him:
We heard him say: I will destroy this temple made by human hands, and in three days I will build another, not made by human hands.
But even then their stories did not agree.
Then the high priest stood up in front of them and asked Jesus: Have you no answer? What is this they are saying against you?
But Jesus stayed silent and gave no answer. Again the high priest asked him: Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed One?
Jesus said: I am. And you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of the Mighty One and coming on the clouds of heaven.
The high priest tore his own clothes and said: Why do we need any more witnesses?
You have heard him insult God. What do you think? They all said he was guilty and must die.
Then some of them began to spit on him. They covered his face, hit him with their fists, and said: Show us you are a prophet! And the guards beat him.
Peter was below in the courtyard. One of the servant girls of the high priest came by.
When she saw Peter warming himself, she looked closely at him and said: You also were with Jesus of Nazareth.
But Peter denied it. He said: I don't know what you are talking about. Then he went out to the entrance, and a rooster crowed.
The servant girl saw him there and again said to the people standing around: This man is one of them.
Again Peter denied it. A little later, those standing nearby said to Peter: Surely you are one of them, because you are from Galilee.
Then Peter began to call down curses on himself, and he swore to them: I do not know this man you are talking about.

At once the rooster crowed the second time. Then Peter remembered what Jesus had said to him: Before the rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you do not know me. And he broke down and cried.

15.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Very early in the morning, the chief priests, the elders, the teachers of the law, and the whole council made their decision. They tied Jesus up, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate.
Pilate asked him: Are you the King of the Jews? Jesus answered: Yes, it is as you say.
The chief priests accused him of many things.
So Pilate asked him again: Have you no answer? Look how many things they are accusing you of.
But Jesus still said nothing, and Pilate was amazed.
Now at every Passover feast, Pilate would set free one prisoner that the people asked for.
A man named Barabbas was in prison with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
The crowd came and asked Pilate to do what he usually did for them.
Will you have me set free the King of the Jews? Pilate asked,
because he knew that the chief priests had handed Jesus over because they were jealous.
But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have Pilate set Barabbas free instead.
Pilate asked them again: Then what should I do with the one you call the King of the Jews?
They shouted: Nail him to a cross!
Pilate asked: Why? What wrong has he done? But they shouted even louder: Nail him to a cross!
Pilate wanted to please the crowd. So he set Barabbas free. He had Jesus beaten with whips and handed him over to be nailed to a cross.
The soldiers led Jesus away into the palace, called the Praetorium, and called the whole group of soldiers together.
They put a purple robe on him and made a crown out of thorns and put it on his head.
Then they began to mock him: Hail, King of the Jews!
Again and again they hit him on the head with a stick and spit on him. They knelt down and pretended to worship him.
After they had mocked him, they took off the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to nail him to a cross.
A man named Simon from Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus, was passing by on his way in from the country. The soldiers forced him to carry the cross.
They brought Jesus to a place called Golgotha (which means The Place of the Skull).
There they offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he would not take it.
Then they nailed him to the cross. They divided his clothes among themselves, rolling dice to decide who would get what.
It was nine o'clock in the morning when they nailed him to the cross.
A written notice of the charge against him said: THE KING OF THE JEWS.
They also nailed two robbers to crosses with him, one on his right and one on his left.
And the Scripture came true that says: He was counted as one of the criminals.
People passing by insulted him, shaking their heads. They said: So! You were going to destroy the temple and build it again in three days?
Come down from the cross and save yourself!
The chief priests and the teachers of the law also mocked him among themselves. They said: He saved others, but he cannot save himself.
Let the Christ, the King of Israel, come down now from the cross, so we can see it and believe. Even the men who were nailed up with him insulted him.
At noon, darkness came over the whole land until three o'clock in the afternoon.
At three o'clock Jesus cried out in a loud voice: Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani? This means: My God, my God, why have you left me?
When some of the people standing nearby heard this, they said: Listen, he is calling Elijah.
One man ran and filled a sponge with sour wine. He put it on a stick and offered it to Jesus to drink. He said: Leave him alone. Let us see if Elijah comes to take him down.
With a loud cry, Jesus took his last breath.
And the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.
The officer standing in front of the cross saw how Jesus died. He said: This man really was the Son of God!
Some women were watching from a distance. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.
These women had followed Jesus and cared for him while he was in Galilee. Many other women who had come up with him to Jerusalem were also there.
It was now evening. The next day was the Sabbath, so this day was the day to get ready for it.
Joseph from Arimathea, a respected member of the council who was also waiting for God's kingdom, bravely went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
Pilate was surprised to hear that Jesus was already dead. He called the officer and asked if Jesus had already died.
When the officer told him it was so, Pilate gave the body to Joseph.
Joseph bought some linen cloth. He took the body down, wrapped it in the linen, and laid it in a tomb cut out of rock. Then he rolled a stone against the entrance of the tomb.
Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where the body was laid.

16.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought sweet spices so they could go and put them on Jesus' body.
Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they went to the tomb.
They asked each other: Who will roll away the stone from the entrance of the tomb for us?
But when they looked up, they saw that the stone had already been rolled away, even though it was very large.
They went into the tomb and saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting on the right side, and they were afraid.
But he said to them: Do not be afraid. You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was nailed to a cross. He has risen! He is not here. Look, here is the place where they laid him.
Now go and tell his disciples and Peter: He is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see him, just as he told you.
The women went out and ran from the tomb, shaking and confused. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid.
After Jesus rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, the woman from whom he had driven out seven demons.
She went and told those who had been with Jesus, who were now crying and full of sorrow.
But when they heard that Jesus was alive and that she had seen him, they did not believe it.
After this, Jesus appeared in a different form to two of them as they were walking out into the country.
They went back and told the others, but they did not believe them either.
Later Jesus appeared to the eleven while they were eating. He corrected them for their lack of faith and their hard hearts, because they had not believed those who saw him after he had risen.
He said to them: Go into all the world and preach the good news to everyone.
Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be judged guilty.
These signs will go with those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons and speak in new languages.
They will pick up snakes with their hands, and if they drink anything deadly, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on sick people, and they will get well.
After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, he was taken up into heaven and sat at the right side of God.
Then the disciples went out and preached everywhere. The Lord worked with them and proved that his word was true by the signs that went with it. Amen.
